programs.name: SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program)
programs.description: Provides monthly benefits to help low-income households buy the food they need for good health. Illinois specific rules apply.

eligibility.work_requirements: ABAWDs (18-64) must work, volunteer, or train 80 hours/month. If unmet, limited to 3 months of benefits in a 3-year period (2024-2026). Exemptions: pregnant, disabled, or caring for child under 14.

programs.apply_in_person: Local IDHS Family Community Resource Center

programs.name: WIC (Women, Infants, and Children)
programs.description: Supplemental foods, health care referrals, and nutrition education for low-income pregnant, breastfeeding, and non-breastfeeding postpartum women, and to infants and children up to age 5.

eligibility.target_demographics: Pregnant, postpartum (up to 6mo, or 1yr if breastfeeding), infants, children up to age 5
eligibility.medical_requirement: Must have a nutritional risk determined by a health professional

programs.name: Illinois All Kids (Medicaid for Children)
programs.description: Comprehensive health insurance program for children in Illinois, covering doctor visits, hospital stays, dental, and vision.
programs.apply_in_person: Local IDHS office or All Kids Application Agent

programs.name: LIHEAP (Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program)
programs.description: Helps eligible low-income households pay for winter energy services.

eligibility.requirement: Must be responsible for home energy costs

programs.name: TANF (Temporary Assistance for Needy Families)
programs.description: Temporary financial assistance for pregnant women and families with one or more dependent children.

eligibility.target_demographics: Families with dependent children or pregnant women
eligibility.work_requirements: Must participate in employment and training activities unless exempt

programs.name: Chicago Public Schools Free School Meals
programs.description: Provides free breakfast and lunch to all enrolled students under the Community Eligibility Provision (CEP).

eligibility.requirement: Must be an enrolled student in a participating Chicago Public School

programs.application_url: No application required for CEP schools

programs.name: Greater Chicago Food Depository Network
programs.description: Network of local food pantries, soup kitchens, and emergency food programs across Cook County.

eligibility.income_limits: Varies by pantry, many offer emergency food with no strict income verification
